project.name: Family Tree
project.description: A full-stack family tree and legacy platform for building, visualizing, and managing generational family history, rendering interactive hierarchical trees with React Flow and Dagre atop a normalized PostgreSQL backend. It includes role-based collaboration, member profiles with linked memory timelines, GEDCOM import, and optional AI-assisted biography generation.

nodes.name: db.js
nodes.summary: Configures and exports a shared PostgreSQL connection pool using either a DATABASE_URL or discrete DB_* environment variables, with connect/error event logging.

nodes.name: authController.js
nodes.summary: Express controller handling user registration and login HTTP requests, delegating business logic to authService and mapping errors to appropriate HTTP status codes.

nodes.name: familyController.js
nodes.summary: Express controller providing full CRUD operations (create, list, get, update, delete) for family records, scoped to the authenticated user.

nodes.name: legacyController.js
nodes.summary: Express controller for creating and listing legacy messages (memorial/tribute text) attached to a person.

nodes.name: reminderCron.js
nodes.summary: Daily scheduled cron job (node-cron) that queries birthdays, death anniversaries, and memory events occurring today across all families and logs mock reminder emails to the console.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a single SQL query with UNION ALL to combine three distinct event sources (birthdays, death anniversaries, memories) into one result set.

nodes.name: errorHandler.js
nodes.summary: Centralized Express error-handling middleware used as the final middleware in the request pipeline to log and format error responses.

nodes.name: validationMiddleware.js
nodes.summary: Reusable Express middleware that runs express-validator's validation result check and rejects invalid requests with a 400 response.

nodes.name: migrate_demo.js
nodes.summary: Standalone Node.js script that seeds or resets the demo user's password hash directly in the database, intended to be run manually or during deployment.

nodes.name: familyModel.js
nodes.summary: Data access layer for the families table, providing parameterized SQL queries for create, read, update, and delete operations scoped to the owning user.

nodes.name: userModel.js
nodes.summary: Data access layer for the users table, providing functions to create users and look them up by email or ID.

nodes.name: authRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Defines the /api/auth Express routes for registration and login, wiring express-validator rules and the validation middleware to the auth controller.

nodes.name: familyRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Defines the /api/families Express routes for family CRUD operations, protected by the verifyToken auth middleware, plus a mock invite endpoint.

nodes.name: legacyRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Defines the /api/legacy Express routes for creating and listing legacy messages for a given person.

nodes.name: personRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Defines the /api/persons Express routes including person CRUD, search, and a multer-based photo upload endpoint with a dedicated rate limiter.

nodes.name: server.js
nodes.summary: Main Express application entry point that configures security middleware (helmet, CORS, rate limiting, sanitization), mounts all API route modules, initializes the reminder cron job, and starts the HTTP server.
nodes.languageNotes: Layers multiple security concerns (helmet CSP/HSTS, CORS origin whitelist, mongo-sanitize, xss-clean, tiered rate limiters) before mounting ten separate route modules.

nodes.name: authService.js
nodes.summary: Business logic layer for authentication: registers new users with bcrypt password hashing and validates login credentials while issuing signed JWT tokens.

nodes.name: familyService.js
nodes.summary: Thin service layer that delegates family CRUD operations to the family model, sitting between the family controller and the data access layer.

nodes.name: setup_demo_user.js
nodes.summary: Standalone Node.js script that seeds a demo user account into the database if one does not already exist.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Handles user registration API requests, validating input body and delegating to authService before returning the new user or an appropriate error status.

nodes.name: login
nodes.summary: Handles user login requests, delegating credential verification to authService and returning a JWT token and user data on success.

nodes.name: createFamily
nodes.summary: Creates a new family record for the authenticated user by validating input and delegating to familyService.

nodes.name: getFamilies
nodes.summary: Retrieves all families belonging to the authenticated user.

nodes.name: getFamilyById
nodes.summary: Fetches a single family by ID, returning 404 if not found or not owned by the requesting user.

nodes.name: updateFamily
nodes.summary: Updates a family's details after validating the request body, scoped to the requesting user.

nodes.name: deleteFamily
nodes.summary: Deletes a family record owned by the authenticated user.

nodes.name: createLegacyMessage
nodes.summary: Creates a legacy message (a written memory or tribute) tied to a person, storing title, message body, and emotion tag.

nodes.name: getLegacyMessages
nodes.summary: Retrieves all legacy messages for a given person, ordered by creation date descending.

nodes.name: errorHandler
nodes.summary: Centralized Express error-handling middleware that logs errors and returns a JSON error response with the appropriate status code.

nodes.name: validateRequest
nodes.summary: Express middleware that checks express-validator results and short-circuits with a 400 response when validation errors are present.

nodes.name: migrate
nodes.summary: One-off migration routine that creates or updates the demo user's password hash in the users table, then exits the process.

nodes.name: createFamily
nodes.summary: Inserts a new family row into the database and returns the created record.

nodes.name: getFamiliesByUser
nodes.summary: Queries all families created by a given user.

nodes.name: getFamilyById
nodes.summary: Queries a single family by ID, scoped to the owning user.

nodes.name: updateFamily
nodes.summary: Updates a family's name in the database, scoped to the owning user.

nodes.name: deleteFamily
nodes.summary: Deletes a family row from the database, scoped to the owning user.

nodes.name: createUser
nodes.summary: Inserts a new user record with a pre-hashed password into the users table.

nodes.name: getUserByEmail
nodes.summary: Looks up a user record by email address.

nodes.name: getUserById
nodes.summary: Looks up a user record by primary key ID.

nodes.name: registerUser
nodes.summary: Business logic for user registration: checks for an existing email, hashes the password with bcrypt, and creates the user record.

nodes.name: loginUser
nodes.summary: Business logic for user login: verifies credentials with bcrypt and issues a signed JWT token on success.

nodes.name: createFamily
nodes.summary: Thin service wrapper delegating family creation to the family model.

nodes.name: getFamiliesByUser
nodes.summary: Thin service wrapper delegating retrieval of a user's families to the family model.

nodes.name: getFamilyById
nodes.summary: Thin service wrapper delegating single-family lookup to the family model.

nodes.name: updateFamily
nodes.summary: Thin service wrapper delegating family updates to the family model.

nodes.name: deleteFamily
nodes.summary: Thin service wrapper delegating family deletion to the family model.

nodes.name: MemoryTimeline.js
nodes.summary: Renders a chronologically sorted, scroll-reveal-animated timeline of memories for a person, falling back to a smart empty-state when no memories exist.

nodes.name: PersonNode.js
nodes.summary: Custom React Flow node component that renders a single person's card in the tree canvas, with generation-based color coding, avatar, lifespan, hover actions, and connection handles.

nodes.name: SearchBar.js
nodes.summary: Global debounced search input that queries persons via the API, shows a results dropdown, and navigates to the selected person's profile with keyboard and outside-click handling.

nodes.name: SmartSuggestions.js
nodes.summary: Analyzes the current tree's nodes and edges to generate dismissible, actionable suggestions such as missing relationships or incomplete profile data.

nodes.name: UpcomingReminders.js
nodes.summary: Fetches upcoming birthday and anniversary events from the API and displays them as a reminders widget.

nodes.name: FamilyTreePage.js
nodes.summary: Main workspace page that assembles the family tree canvas with the member/relationship creation forms, activity feed, insights panel, collaboration panel, search bar, and GEDCOM import, and owns tree data fetching plus create/update handlers.

nodes.name: PersonProfile.js
nodes.summary: Large person profile page combining editable fields, avatar upload, a mini tree preview, family relationship management, memory and legacy-message CRUD, privacy/sharing controls, and AI-assisted biography and story-mode generation.

nodes.name: familyService.js
nodes.summary: Service layer wrapping REST calls for families, persons, and relationships (create/read/update/delete) on top of the shared Axios API client.

nodes.name: TimelineCard
nodes.summary: Renders a single memory as an animated timeline entry, revealed via IntersectionObserver, showing date and tags.

nodes.name: SmartEmptyState
nodes.summary: Empty-state placeholder shown when a person has no recorded memories, with animated skeleton placeholders.

nodes.name: MemoryTimeline
nodes.summary: Default-exported component that sorts memories chronologically and renders TimelineCard entries or the SmartEmptyState.

nodes.name: PersonNode
nodes.summary: Memoized React Flow node component rendering a person's avatar, name, lifespan, gender/occupation/birthplace chips, generation-based color styling, and hover-triggered quick-add-relative buttons and tooltip.

nodes.name: GlobalSearchBar
nodes.summary: Default-exported global search component: debounces query input, fetches matching persons from the API, manages a results dropdown with outside-click and keyboard navigation, and navigates to the selected profile.

nodes.name: SmartSuggestions
nodes.summary: Default-exported component that derives actionable suggestions (missing parents/spouses, incomplete profiles) from the current tree's nodes and edges, and renders a dismissible suggestion list.

nodes.name: UpcomingReminders
nodes.summary: Default-exported component that fetches upcoming birthday/anniversary events on mount and renders them as a categorized reminders list.

nodes.name: WorkspaceSummary
nodes.summary: Computes workspace-level summary statistics (member count, max generation depth via recursive getDepth, relationship type breakdown) and renders them as stat cards.

nodes.name: SmartMemberForm
nodes.summary: Smart add-member form that detects likely duplicate names against existing tree members and previews a gender-based icon while the user types.

nodes.name: RelationshipBuilder
nodes.summary: Form for selecting two existing persons and a relationship type/label to create a new relationship between them.

nodes.name: FamilyTreePage
nodes.summary: Default-exported top-level workspace page: resolves the selected family from the URL/localStorage, fetches the tree via familyService, and wires create-person/create-relationship handlers plus the activity log across all child panels.

nodes.name: Avatar
nodes.summary: Renders a circular avatar image, falling back to the person's initial letter when no photo is set.

nodes.name: UploadableAvatar
nodes.summary: Avatar variant supporting click-to-upload with local preview and a direct fetch-based upload request, invoking a callback once the new photo URL is available.

nodes.name: Chip
nodes.summary: Small presentational pill/tag UI element used throughout the profile page.

nodes.name: BreadcrumbNav
nodes.summary: Renders breadcrumb navigation linking back to the dashboard and family tree.

nodes.name: InlineEditField
nodes.summary: Inline click-to-edit text field that commits changes on blur or Enter and reverts on Escape.

nodes.name: useCountUp
nodes.summary: Custom hook that animates a numeric value counting up to a target over a fixed interval.

nodes.name: StatCard
nodes.summary: Presentational stat card that displays an animated metric using the useCountUp hook.

nodes.name: InsightsPanel
nodes.summary: Displays computed profile insights such as age and derived stats using StatCard.

nodes.name: ProfileStrengthBar
nodes.summary: Computes profile completeness from a set of field checks and renders a progress bar with hints about missing fields.

nodes.name: StoryModeModal
nodes.summary: Modal that requests an AI-generated narrative summary ('story mode') of the person from the API and displays it with a loading skeleton.

nodes.name: MiniTreePreview
nodes.summary: Renders a small SVG-based mini family tree preview showing the current person's parents, children, and spouses with click-to-navigate nodes.

nodes.name: PrivacyControls
nodes.summary: Controls for toggling a profile's public/private visibility, copying a share link to the clipboard, and exporting/printing the profile.

nodes.name: FamilyMemberCard
nodes.summary: Card summarizing a related family member with click-to-navigate, edit, and delete relationship actions.

nodes.name: FamilySection
nodes.summary: Groups a person's relationships by resolved role (parent/child/spouse/sibling) and renders grouped FamilyMemberCard lists.

nodes.name: MemoryForm
nodes.summary: Form for composing a new memory entry with title, description, date, tag toggles, and emotion selection.

nodes.name: BiographySection
nodes.summary: Displays the person's biography and triggers AI-assisted biography generation via the API, showing a generation-in-progress skeleton.

nodes.name: PersonProfile
nodes.summary: Default-exported top-level profile page: fetches person, relationships, memories, and legacy data in parallel, manages inline/full editing, memory and legacy-message CRUD, relationship edit/delete, and composes all profile sub-sections.

nodes.name: createFamily
nodes.summary: Creates a new family via POST /families and normalizes the response payload.

nodes.name: getFamilies
nodes.summary: Fetches all families for the current user via GET /families.

nodes.name: createPerson
nodes.summary: Creates a new person via POST /persons.

nodes.name: createRelationship
nodes.summary: Creates a new relationship between two persons via POST /relationships.

nodes.name: getFamilyTree
nodes.summary: Fetches the aggregated tree (nodes and edges) for a family via GET /family-tree/:family_id.

nodes.name: updateFamily
nodes.summary: Updates a family's data via PUT /families/:family_id.

nodes.name: deleteFamily
nodes.summary: Deletes a family via DELETE /families/:family_id.

nodes.name: updatePerson
nodes.summary: Updates a person's data via PUT /persons/:person_id.

nodes.name: deletePerson
nodes.summary: Deletes a person via DELETE /persons/:person_id.

nodes.name: updateRelationship
nodes.summary: Updates a relationship's data via PUT /relationships/:relationship_id.

nodes.name: deleteRelationship
nodes.summary: Deletes a relationship via DELETE /relationships/:relationship_id.

nodes.name: api.js
nodes.summary: Configures the shared Axios HTTP client with a base URL, JWT bearer-token request interceptor, and a global response interceptor that standardizes error messages and redirects to login on 401 responses.
nodes.languageNotes: Central place where all frontend API calls flow through, making it a natural spot for cross-cutting auth/error concerns.

nodes.name: ActivityFeed.js
nodes.summary: Renders a live, auto-scrolling feed of recent collaboration activity events (adds, edits, invites) on the family tree workspace.

nodes.name: CollaborationPanel.js
nodes.summary: Displays the list of family tree collaborators and provides an invite-by-email form plus per-collaborator role change and removal controls.

nodes.name: FamilyInsightsPanel.js
nodes.summary: Computes aggregate statistics about the family tree (member counts, generation depth, relationship breakdown) and renders them as an animated insights dashboard panel.

nodes.name: FamilyInvite.js
nodes.summary: Standalone form component that lets a user invite a new collaborator by email and role, posting the invitation through the API client.

nodes.name: FamilyTimeline.js
nodes.summary: Fetches family-wide chronological events from the API and renders them as a vertical timeline with loading and error states.

nodes.name: FamilyTree.js
nodes.summary: Core interactive family tree visualization built on React Flow: computes a generation-based hierarchical layout (adjacency building, generation assignment, collision resolution, iterative barycenter positioning) and renders the pannable/zoomable tree canvas with focus mode, search highlighting, and PNG export.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a custom Dagre-like iterative layout algorithm (generation BFS + collision resolution + barycenter smoothing) rather than relying solely on an external layout library.

nodes.name: GedcomImport.js
nodes.summary: Drag-and-drop and click-to-browse GEDCOM file uploader with client-side validation, upload progress tracking, and API submission for bulk family data import.

nodes.name: LegacySection.js
nodes.summary: Displays a person's legacy/memory messages and provides a composer form (title, message, emotion tag) for adding new legacy entries.

nodes.name: ActivityItem
nodes.summary: Renders a single activity feed entry with an entrance animation and relative timestamp.

nodes.name: ActivityFeed
nodes.summary: Default-exported component that renders the scrollable, reverse-chronological list of activity items and auto-scrolls to the newest entry.

nodes.name: CollaborationPanel
nodes.summary: Main collaboration panel component: manages collaborator state, handles invite submission via the API, and lets the current user change a collaborator's role or remove them.

nodes.name: AnimatedCounter
nodes.summary: Reusable counter component that animates from zero to a target numeric value using requestAnimationFrame with an easing curve.

nodes.name: InsightCard
nodes.summary: Presentational card that displays a single labeled insight metric with an icon.

nodes.name: FamilyInsightsPanel
nodes.summary: Default-exported component that derives tree-wide statistics (generation counts, relationship totals, completeness) from nodes/edges via useMemo and renders the insights dashboard.

nodes.name: FamilyInvite
nodes.summary: Default-exported standalone invite form managing email/role state and submitting the invitation to the API.

nodes.name: FamilyTimeline
nodes.summary: Default-exported component that fetches family timeline events on mount and renders them as a dated vertical list, handling loading and error states.

nodes.name: buildAdjacency
nodes.summary: Builds parent-of, child-of, and same-generation adjacency maps from React Flow nodes and edges, used as the foundation for layout computation.

nodes.name: assignGenerations
nodes.summary: Assigns a generation level to every person node via multi-directional BFS traversal from root ancestors, propagating through parent, child, and same-generation links.

nodes.name: initialXPositions
nodes.summary: Computes an initial evenly-spaced horizontal x-position for each node within its generation level.

nodes.name: resolveCollisions
nodes.summary: Adjusts x-positions of nodes within a single generation to enforce a minimum horizontal gap and eliminate overlaps.

nodes.name: iterativeLayout
nodes.summary: Iteratively refines node x-positions top-down (from parents) and bottom-up (from children) using barycenter averaging, then resolves collisions per generation.

nodes.name: computeLayout
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full tree layout pipeline: builds adjacency, assigns generations, groups nodes, computes initial positions, runs iterative layout, and resolves final collisions to produce positioned React Flow nodes.

nodes.name: LayoutUpdater
nodes.summary: Helper component that calls React Flow's fitView after layout changes settle, using a debounced timeout.

nodes.name: QuickAddForm
nodes.summary: Inline quick-add form allowing a new person's name to be entered directly on the tree canvas.

nodes.name: FamilyTreeInner
nodes.summary: Main tree canvas component: manages React Flow node/edge state, computes focus/hover/search highlighting, wires up node click and quick-add handlers, and implements PNG export of the visible tree.

nodes.name: FamilyTree
nodes.summary: Default-exported public wrapper that provides the ReactFlowProvider context around FamilyTreeInner.

nodes.name: GedcomImport
nodes.summary: Default-exported component implementing drag-and-drop and click-to-browse GEDCOM file selection, client-side extension validation, upload progress tracking via the API, and success/error messaging.

nodes.name: LegacyCard
nodes.summary: Renders a single expandable legacy/memory message card with a formatted date and expand/collapse toggle.

nodes.name: LegacySection
nodes.summary: Default-exported component managing the legacy message composer form (title, message, emotion) and rendering the list of LegacyCard entries for a person.

nodes.name: App.js
nodes.summary: Root React component that sets up the router, lazy-loaded route pages, global toast provider, and an online/offline network status listener.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses React.lazy/Suspense to code-split all page routes (Register, Login, Dashboard, FamilyTreePage, PersonProfile, StoryTransition) for smaller initial bundle size.

nodes.name: NetworkListener
nodes.summary: Invisible component that listens for browser online/offline events and surfaces connectivity status via toast notifications.

nodes.name: PageLoader
nodes.summary: Simple full-screen spinner shown as the Suspense fallback while lazy-loaded route pages are fetched.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Top-level application component wiring ToastProvider, BrowserRouter, and all page routes with a catch-all redirect to login.

nodes.name: App.test.js
nodes.summary: Default Create-React-App smoke test that renders the App component and checks for a 'learn react' link.

nodes.name: CanvasNetwork.js
nodes.summary: Decorative canvas component that animates a network of connected particles as an ambient background effect for auth and dashboard pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses HTML5 Canvas 2D API with requestAnimationFrame for a particle network animation, cleaning up listeners and animation frames on unmount.

nodes.name: CanvasNetwork
nodes.summary: Renders and drives an animated particle-network canvas background, handling resize, particle initialization, and per-frame drawing.

nodes.name: Footer.js
nodes.summary: Site-wide footer displaying the current year copyright notice, hidden on the full-screen StoryTransition route.

nodes.name: Footer
nodes.summary: Renders the app footer with copyright text, suppressing itself on routes listed in HIDDEN_ROUTES.

nodes.name: ToastContext.js
nodes.summary: React context provider implementing a global toast notification system, exposing addToast/removeToast via the useToast hook.

nodes.name: useToast
nodes.summary: Custom hook that returns the ToastContext value (addToast function) for any consuming component.

nodes.name: ToastProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider component that manages toast state, auto-dismisses toasts after 4 seconds, and renders the toast stack UI.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Application bootstrap that mounts the root App component into the DOM, suppresses a benign ResizeObserver console error, and initiates web-vitals reporting.

nodes.name: Dashboard.js
nodes.summary: Main authenticated dashboard page listing the user's families as animated lineage cards, with create, rename, and delete family workflows.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines two canvas-based decorative sub-components (CardLineageCanvas, particle network per card) with extensive local state for modals, loading, and family CRUD operations.

nodes.name: CardLineageCanvas
nodes.summary: Per-card animated canvas rendering a small network of connected nodes as decorative background for each family lineage card.

nodes.name: LineageCard
nodes.summary: Renders a single family card with hover state, formatted creation date, and select/update/delete actions.

nodes.name: Dashboard
nodes.summary: Top-level dashboard page component that fetches families on mount, tracks mouse position for background effects, and orchestrates create/rename/delete family modals.

nodes.name: Login.js
nodes.summary: Login page with an animated canvas-network background that authenticates the user against the backend and stores the returned JWT token.

nodes.name: Login
nodes.summary: Renders the login form, submits credentials to the auth API, stores the JWT in localStorage on success, and surfaces errors via toast.

nodes.name: Register.js
nodes.summary: Registration page that collects name, email, and password, submits a signup request to the backend, and redirects to login on success.

nodes.name: Register
nodes.summary: Renders the registration form, posts new-account data to the auth API, and handles duplicate-email and validation error states via toast.

nodes.name: reportWebVitals.js
nodes.summary: Create-React-App performance reporting utility that lazily loads web-vitals and forwards CLS/FID/FCP/LCP/TTFB metrics to a callback.

nodes.name: reportWebVitals
nodes.summary: Dynamically imports the web-vitals library and, if a callback is provided, reports each core web vital metric to it.

nodes.name: components.test.js
nodes.summary: Integration-style tests rendering the Login, Register, and Dashboard pages inside a BrowserRouter to assert key UI text and form fields appear.

nodes.name: aiController.js
nodes.summary: Express controller handling AI-assisted biography generation for a person, orchestrating calls to person and relationship services and persisting the generated bio back to the person's profile.
nodes.languageNotes: AI generation is currently a placeholder that mocks the biography text rather than calling an external LLM API.

nodes.name: personController.js
nodes.summary: Express controller exposing CRUD and search HTTP endpoints for person records, delegating persistence logic to personService and translating results into HTTP status codes and error responses.

nodes.name: relationshipController.js
nodes.summary: Express controller managing relationship records between family members (create, fetch, update, delete), delegating persistence to relationshipService.

nodes.name: personModel.js
nodes.summary: Data access layer for the persons table, providing parameterized SQL queries for creating, reading, updating, deleting, and searching person records.
nodes.languageNotes: updatePerson dynamically builds its SET clause from only the fields present in the input object, avoiding overwriting unspecified columns.

nodes.name: relationshipModel.js
nodes.summary: Data access layer for the relationships table, providing parameterized SQL queries to create, fetch, update, and delete relationships between persons.

nodes.name: aiRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Express router wiring the protected AI biography generation endpoint to aiController, guarded by token verification middleware.

nodes.name: relationshipRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Express router defining protected CRUD endpoints for relationships, delegating to relationshipController and enforcing authentication via middleware.

nodes.name: personService.js
nodes.summary: Service layer wrapping personModel to provide business logic for person CRUD operations and name search, consumed by multiple controllers across the app.

nodes.name: relationshipService.js
nodes.summary: Service layer wrapping relationshipModel to provide business logic for creating, fetching, updating, and deleting relationships between family members.

nodes.name: generateBiography
nodes.summary: Handles POST /api/ai/generate-biography — fetches a person and their relationships, builds a mocked AI-generated biography string, saves it back to the person, and returns it in the response.

nodes.name: addPerson
nodes.summary: Handles POST request to create a new person, delegating to personService.addPerson and returning the created record or a validation/server error.

nodes.name: getPersonsByFamily
nodes.summary: Handles GET request to list all persons belonging to a specific family.

nodes.name: getPersonById
nodes.summary: Handles GET request to fetch a single person by ID, returning 404 if the person does not exist.

nodes.name: updatePerson
nodes.summary: Handles PUT request to update a person's details via personService, returning 404 if the person is not found.

nodes.name: deletePerson
nodes.summary: Handles DELETE request to remove a person record, returning 404 if the person is not found.

nodes.name: searchPersons
nodes.summary: Handles GET search request, validating the query string parameter and delegating to personService.searchPersons.

nodes.name: createRelationship
nodes.summary: Handles POST request to create a relationship between two persons, delegating to relationshipService with validation error handling.

nodes.name: getRelationshipsByPerson
nodes.summary: Handles GET request to list all relationships involving a given person.

nodes.name: deleteRelationship
nodes.summary: Handles DELETE request to remove a relationship by ID, returning 404 if not found.

nodes.name: updateRelationship
nodes.summary: Handles PUT request to update a relationship's type, validating input and returning 404 if the relationship does not exist.

nodes.name: createPerson
nodes.summary: Inserts a new person row into the persons table using a parameterized INSERT query and returns the created record.

nodes.name: getPersonsByFamily
nodes.summary: Selects all persons belonging to a given family, ordered by birth date.

nodes.name: getPersonById
nodes.summary: Selects a single person row by ID.

nodes.name: updatePerson
nodes.summary: Dynamically builds and executes an UPDATE query for only the fields present in the provided data object, returning the updated row.

nodes.name: deletePerson
nodes.summary: Deletes a person row by ID and returns whether a row was removed.

nodes.name: searchPersons
nodes.summary: Performs a case-insensitive ILIKE search across first/last name fields, limited to 20 results.

nodes.name: createRelationship
nodes.summary: Inserts a new relationship row linking two persons with a given relationship type.

nodes.name: getRelationshipsByPerson
nodes.summary: Selects all relationship rows involving a given person ID.

nodes.name: deleteRelationship
nodes.summary: Deletes a relationship row by ID.

nodes.name: updateRelationship
nodes.summary: Updates the relationship_type field for a given relationship ID.

nodes.name: addPerson
nodes.summary: Delegates person creation to personModel.createPerson.

nodes.name: getPersonsByFamily
nodes.summary: Delegates family-scoped person retrieval to personModel.getPersonsByFamily.

nodes.name: getPersonById
nodes.summary: Delegates single person lookup to personModel.getPersonById.

nodes.name: updatePerson
nodes.summary: Delegates person update to personModel.updatePerson.

nodes.name: deletePerson
nodes.summary: Delegates person deletion to personModel.deletePerson.

nodes.name: searchPersons
nodes.summary: Trims the query string and delegates name search to personModel.searchPersons.

nodes.name: createRelationship
nodes.summary: Delegates relationship creation to relationshipModel.createRelationship.

nodes.name: getRelationshipsByPerson
nodes.summary: Delegates relationship lookup by person to relationshipModel.getRelationshipsByPerson.

nodes.name: deleteRelationship
nodes.summary: Delegates relationship deletion to relationshipModel.deleteRelationship.

nodes.name: updateRelationship
nodes.summary: Delegates relationship type update to relationshipModel.updateRelationship.

nodes.name: gedcomController.js
nodes.summary: Express controller handling GEDCOM file import: parses the uploaded .ged file, deduplicates and inserts persons/relationships into Postgres inside a single transaction, then returns the rebuilt family tree data for the frontend.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a single Postgres client transaction (BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK) to atomically insert parsed GEDCOM persons and relationships, with per-row duplicate checks against name/birth_date.

nodes.name: importGedcom
nodes.summary: Async Express handler that reads an uploaded GEDCOM file, parses it, deduplicates and inserts persons/relationships into Postgres inside a transaction, and returns the rebuilt tree data required by the frontend.

nodes.name: reminderController.js
nodes.summary: Express controller that fetches upcoming birthdays, death anniversaries, and memory events for a family via a UNION SQL query, computes each event's next yearly occurrence, and returns those within 45 days sorted by proximity.

nodes.name: getUpcomingReminders
nodes.summary: Queries persons and memories tables via UNION ALL to gather birthday, death-anniversary, and memory events for a family, computes each event's next yearly occurrence in JS, and filters to events within 45 days.

nodes.name: authMiddleware.js
nodes.summary: Express middleware that verifies the JWT bearer token from the Authorization header, attaches the decoded user payload to the request, and rejects unauthenticated or invalid requests.

nodes.name: verifyToken
nodes.summary: Validates the Bearer JWT from the Authorization header using JWT_SECRET, attaches the decoded payload to req.user, and calls next() on success or returns 401 on failure or missing token.

nodes.name: familyTreeRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Express router exposing a protected GET endpoint that returns ReactFlow-formatted nodes/edges for a family's tree by delegating to treeBuilder.

nodes.name: gedcomRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Express router configuring Multer disk storage and a .ged file filter (5MB limit) for a protected GEDCOM import endpoint that delegates to gedcomController.importGedcom, with manual Multer error handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Multer diskStorage with a custom fileFilter restricting uploads to .ged files under 5MB, and wraps the upload middleware call to catch MulterError before invoking the controller.

nodes.name: reminderRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Minimal Express router exposing a single protected GET endpoint that returns upcoming reminders for a family via reminderController.

nodes.name: gedcomParser.js
nodes.summary: Utility that parses raw GEDCOM file content into structured person records and spouse/parent-child relationships by walking INDI and FAM node trees.
nodes.languageNotes: Parses the GEDCOM hierarchical tag tree (INDI/FAM/NAME/BIRT/etc.) using the third-party `gedcom` package, manually splitting the GEDCOM NAME field on '/' to separate first and last names.

nodes.name: parseGedcom
nodes.summary: Parses a raw GEDCOM string into arrays of person objects (name, gender, birth date/place) and relationship objects (spouse, parent-child) by iterating INDI and FAM tag trees.

nodes.name: treeBuilder.js
nodes.summary: Utility that queries persons and relationships for a family from Postgres and transforms them into ReactFlow-compatible nodes and edges, normalizing relationship direction into a top-down parent-to-child DAG.
nodes.languageNotes: Normalizes both 'parent' and 'child' relationship rows into a consistent top-down 'Parent' edge direction so the frontend Dagre layout renders a proper generational hierarchy.

nodes.name: buildFamilyTreeData
nodes.summary: Fetches persons and relationships for a family_id from Postgres and maps them into ReactFlow node/edge objects, flipping 'child' relationships to a consistent top-down 'Parent' edge label.

nodes.name: eventController.js
nodes.summary: Controller that builds a unified, chronologically sorted family timeline by combining birth/death events derived from person records with linked memory entries.
nodes.languageNotes: Silently swallows per-person memory fetch errors inside the aggregation loop to keep building the timeline even if one lookup fails.

nodes.name: getFamilyEvents
nodes.summary: Fetches all persons for a family, derives birth/death events, appends each person's memories that have an event date, then returns the combined list sorted chronologically ascending.

nodes.name: memoryController.js
nodes.summary: Express controller exposing HTTP handlers for family memories — create, list by family, list by person, and delete — delegating all business logic to memoryService.

nodes.name: createMemory
nodes.summary: Handles POST requests to create a new memory, forwarding the request body to memoryService and returning a 400 for validation errors or 500 for unexpected failures.

nodes.name: getMemoriesByFamily
nodes.summary: Handles GET requests returning all memories associated with a given family_id route parameter.

nodes.name: getMemoriesByPerson
nodes.summary: Handles GET requests returning all memories associated with a given person_id route parameter.

nodes.name: deleteMemory
nodes.summary: Handles DELETE requests removing a memory by id, responding with 404 if the memory was not found and 200 on success.

nodes.name: memoryModel.js
nodes.summary: Data access layer executing parameterized SQL queries against the memories table for create, list-by-family, list-by-person, and delete operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses parameterized queries ($1, $2, ...) with node-postgres to prevent SQL injection.

nodes.name: createMemory
nodes.summary: Inserts a new memory row (family_id, person_id, title, description, media, event_date) and returns the created record.

nodes.name: getMemoriesByFamily
nodes.summary: Selects all memories for a family, ordered by event date descending (nulls last) then creation time.

nodes.name: getMemoriesByPerson
nodes.summary: Selects all memories linked to a specific person, ordered by event date descending (nulls last) then creation time.

nodes.name: deleteMemory
nodes.summary: Deletes a memory row by id and returns whether a row was actually removed.

nodes.name: eventRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Express router wiring the protected GET /api/events endpoint to the eventController's family timeline handler.

nodes.name: memoryRoutes.js
nodes.summary: Express router defining protected CRUD endpoints (create, list by family, list by person, delete) for family memories, delegating to memoryController.

nodes.name: memoryService.js
nodes.summary: Business logic layer validating memory input (required family_id/title/person_id/memory_id) before delegating persistence operations to memoryModel.

nodes.name: createMemory
nodes.summary: Validates that family_id and title are present before delegating memory creation to memoryModel.

nodes.name: getMemoriesByFamily
nodes.summary: Validates family_id and delegates to memoryModel to fetch memories for a family.

nodes.name: getMemoriesByPerson
nodes.summary: Validates person_id and delegates to memoryModel to fetch memories for a person.

nodes.name: deleteMemory
nodes.summary: Validates memory_id and delegates deletion to memoryModel.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker build for the backend Node.js API, installing production dependencies on a node:18-alpine base image and starting the server with npm start on port 5001.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage build (no multi-stage separation of build vs runtime layers); relies on `npm install --production` to keep the image lean instead.

nodes.name: node
nodes.summary: The sole build stage, based on node:18-alpine, that copies package manifests, installs production dependencies, copies the rest of the backend source, and exposes port 5001 for the API server.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the React frontend: compiles the app with Node.js and serves the resulting static assets through a lightweight Nginx image.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Docker multi-stage builds: a `node:18-alpine` stage installs dependencies and runs `npm run build`, then only the compiled output is copied into a minimal `nginx:alpine` runtime stage, keeping the final image small and free of build tooling.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: First Docker stage (node:18-alpine) that installs npm dependencies and runs `npm run build` to produce the production static assets for the frontend.

nodes.name: nginx
nodes.summary: Second Docker stage (nginx:alpine) that copies the compiled frontend assets from the build stage and serves them on port 80.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project README describing the Family Tree platform's architecture (React Flow + Dagre frontend, Node.js/Express backend, PostgreSQL database), tech stack, local installation and run instructions, environment variables, REST API endpoint reference, and a production deployment guide (Supabase/Neon, Render/Railway, Vercel).

nodes.name: security_audit.md
nodes.summary: Security audit report for the Virasat.ai / Family Tree backend and frontend, cataloging 3 critical, 5 high, and 2 medium severity vulnerabilities that were found and fixed (hardcoded JWT fallback secret, wildcard CORS, leaked error messages, missing XSS sanitization, exposed Swagger UI, weak rate limiting, permissive Helmet config), plus a checklist of recommended pre-production hardening steps.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose configuration that orchestrates the PostgreSQL database, Node.js backend, and React frontend as three networked services for local development and one-command containerized startup.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a shared bridge network (family_net) and a named volume (pgdata) for database persistence; services communicate by service name rather than hardcoded hostnames.

nodes.name: database
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 15 database service storing the family tree relational data, exposing port 5432 and persisting data via the pgdata named volume.

nodes.name: backend
nodes.summary: Node.js/Express backend service built from the ./backend directory, exposing port 5001 and configured via environment variables for DB connection and JWT secret; depends on the database service being available.

nodes.name: frontend
nodes.summary: React frontend service built from the ./frontend directory, served on host port 3000 (mapped to container port 80); depends on the backend service.

nodes.name: .env
nodes.summary: Backend runtime environment file supplying the server port, PostgreSQL connection credentials, JWT signing secret, and allowed CORS origins for the Express server in production mode.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains live-looking values (a real DB password and a 128-character JWT secret) rather than placeholders, which is a notable security concern if this file is committed to version control.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment file documenting the variables required to run the Virasat.ai family tree backend (server port, PostgreSQL connection, JWT secret, CORS origins) with placeholder values and setup instructions.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js package manifest for the Family Tree Express backend, declaring core dependencies (Express, pg, jsonwebtoken, bcryptjs, multer, gedcom, node-cron, helmet, cors) and npm scripts for starting the server in development and production.
nodes.languageNotes: Dependency set indicates a security-hardened Express API (helmet, express-rate-limit, express-mongo-sanitize, xss-clean) combined with GEDCOM genealogy file parsing and scheduled jobs via node-cron.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Stores registered application user accounts with hashed passwords, used for authentication and as the owner reference for created families.

nodes.name: families
nodes.summary: Represents a family unit created by a user, acting as the top-level grouping for persons, memories, and the overall family tree.

nodes.name: persons
nodes.summary: Core entity table storing individual family members with biographical details (name, gender, birth/death dates, birth place, occupation, bio, photo) scoped to a family.

nodes.name: relationships
nodes.summary: Junction table linking two persons via a typed relationship (e.g. parent, spouse), with a check constraint preventing a person from being related to themselves.

nodes.name: memories
nodes.summary: Stores memory and timeline entries (title, description, media, event date) linked to a person and family, powering the member memory timeline feature.

nodes.name: legacy_messages
nodes.summary: Stores legacy/heritage messages authored for a person, optionally tagged with an emotion and referencing associated memory records via an array of memory IDs.

nodes.name: swagger.yaml
nodes.summary: OpenAPI 3.0 specification documenting the Family Tree REST API, covering authentication, family and person management, relationships, GEDCOM import, and family-tree visualization endpoints, served via swagger-ui-express.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses bearer JWT security globally with explicit opt-outs on the register/login endpoints, matching a typical JWT-based auth flow.

nodes.name: POST /api/auth/register
nodes.summary: Registers a new user account with name, email, and password; returns 201 on success.

nodes.name: POST /api/auth/login
nodes.summary: Authenticates a user with email and password credentials, returning a success response used to establish an authenticated session.

nodes.name: GET /api/families
nodes.summary: Retrieves the list of families belonging to the authenticated user.

nodes.name: POST /api/families
nodes.summary: Creates a new family record with a family name for the authenticated user.

nodes.name: GET /api/family-tree/{family_id}
nodes.summary: Returns the family tree structure as nodes and edges formatted for rendering with React Flow.

nodes.name: POST /api/persons
nodes.summary: Adds a new family member (person) with name, gender, and birth place details to a family.

nodes.name: POST /api/relationships
nodes.summary: Creates a relationship link between two family members with a specified relationship type (e.g. parent).

nodes.name: POST /api/gedcom/import
nodes.summary: Imports an uploaded GEDCOM genealogy file and converts it into family tree nodes and edges.

nodes.name: vercel.json
nodes.summary: Vercel deployment configuration that builds the Express server.js entry point as a serverless function and routes all incoming requests to it.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment file for the React frontend, declaring the REACT_APP_API_BASE_URL variable used to point the client at the backend API.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Standard Create React App documentation covering available npm scripts (start, test, build, eject), code splitting, bundle analysis, PWA setup, and deployment guidance.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM manifest for the React frontend, declaring dependencies (React 19, React Router, ReactFlow, Dagre, elkjs, axios, Tailwind CSS, html-to-image) and Create React App scripts for the family tree visualization client.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses react-scripts (Create React App) for tooling, with ReactFlow and Dagre/elkjs paired for interactive hierarchical tree layout and rendering, and Tailwind CSS as a devDependency for styling.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry template for the Create React App-based frontend; mounts the React app into a #root div, sets Virasat.ai branding/meta tags and description, links the PWA manifest and favicon, and includes a Google Analytics boilerplate script with a placeholder tracking ID.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard Create React App public/index.html using %PUBLIC_URL% substitution; the Google Analytics tracking ID is still the unconfigured placeholder 'YOUR_TRACKING_ID'.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: Progressive Web App manifest defining installable app metadata (icons, start URL, display mode, theme/background colors) for the frontend; still contains the default unmodified Create React App boilerplate values.
nodes.languageNotes: short_name and name still read 'React App' / 'Create React App Sample', indicating this manifest was never customized for the Virasat.ai/Family Tree branding used elsewhere in index.html.

nodes.name: robots.txt
nodes.summary: Robots exclusion file for the frontend static site that permits all web crawlers unrestricted access (no Disallow rules).

nodes.name: .DS_Store
nodes.summary: macOS Finder metadata file that stores folder view settings and icon positions; not part of the application source and carries no functional logic.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern configuration for the Understand-Anything analysis tool, listing commented-out gitignore-style globs (test files, generated code, per-language exclusions) that can be uncommented to exclude paths from the knowledge graph scan.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses gitignore-style glob syntax with '!' negation support; all example rules ship commented out as opt-in suggestions.

nodes.name: init_cloud_db.js
nodes.summary: Standalone CLI script that connects to an external/cloud PostgreSQL database via a connection string argument and applies schema.sql to bootstrap the production database (e.g., for a Vercel deployment).

nodes.name: initializeDatabase
nodes.summary: Reads schema.sql from disk and executes it against the cloud Postgres connection pool, logging progress and closing the pool when done.

nodes.name: init_db.js
nodes.summary: Local development setup script that creates the family_tree PostgreSQL database if it does not already exist, then connects to it and applies schema.sql to build the schema.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses hardcoded localhost credentials, appropriate only for local development bootstrapping rather than production use.

nodes.name: setup
nodes.summary: Connects to the default postgres database to conditionally create the family_tree database, then reconnects to family_tree and applies schema.sql.

nodes.name: integration.test.js
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration test suite that exercises the live backend API over HTTP (register, login, create family, add person, create relationship) using supertest against a running local server.
nodes.languageNotes: Tests hit a running dev server via HTTP rather than importing application modules directly, so no static project-internal imports are present.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: PostCSS build configuration enabling the tailwindcss and autoprefixer plugins for the frontend's CSS pipeline.

nodes.name: App.css
nodes.summary: Legacy Create React App boilerplate stylesheet defining the .App container, header, logo spin animation, and link styling.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet that loads Tailwind's base, components, and utilities layers and sets base body/code font families for the app.

nodes.name: StoryTransition.js
nodes.summary: Full-screen cinematic transition page that staggers two fading text lines over timed intervals before auto-navigating to the demo family tree view.

nodes.name: StoryTransition
nodes.summary: React functional component that sequences four timed stages (text fade-ins, screen fade-out, then navigation to '/family-tree?demo=true') using chained setTimeout calls cleaned up on unmount.

nodes.name: setupTests.js
nodes.summary: Jest test environment setup file that imports @testing-library/jest-dom to enable custom DOM matchers for the frontend test suite.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration scanning all JS/JSX/TS/TSX files under src for class usage, with an empty theme extension and no additional plugins.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Express route definitions and controllers that expose the family-tree REST API (auth, families, persons, relationships, events, memories, legacy messages, GEDCOM, AI, reminders), plus the server entry point and integration test.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Backend business-logic modules that orchestrate family/person/relationship/memory/auth operations, the reminder cron job, and the GEDCOM parsing / tree-building domain algorithms.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: PostgreSQL data-access models, the DB connection pool, database setup/seed scripts, the normalized schema.sql table definitions, and the swagger.yaml API contract with its documented endpoints.

layers.name: Middleware Layer
layers.description: Express middleware for JWT authentication, centralized error handling, and request validation shared across the backend API.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React components and pages that render the interactive family tree (React Flow/Dagre canvas), member profiles, memory timelines, collaboration and GEDCOM import UI, plus the app shell, styling, and CRA bootstrap files.

layers.name: Client Services Layer
layers.description: Frontend integration layer that centralizes HTTP calls to the backend REST API and family-specific client-side service logic, consumed across nearly all UI components.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Docker and Docker Compose definitions for backend, frontend, and database containers, and the Vercel deployment configuration for the backend.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Package manifests, environment variable templates, build-tool configs (Tailwind, PostCSS), the PWA manifest, and miscellaneous project metadata files for both backend and frontend.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project-level and frontend README files, the security audit report, and the frontend's robots.txt.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to learn what Family Tree is: a full-stack platform for building and visualizing interactive family trees with React Flow and Dagre, backed by a normalized PostgreSQL database. It lays out the monorepo split (backend/ for the Express API, frontend/ for the React 18 CRA app), the tech stack, and the full REST API surface that the rest of this tour will walk through in code.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point
tour.description: backend/server.js is where the Express API boots up. It configures security middleware (helmet, CORS, rate limiting, sanitization), mounts every route module described in the README's API reference, starts the reminder cron job, and starts the HTTP server. With the highest fan-out of any backend file, it is the single place that wires the entire API together.
tour.languageLesson: Express apps are typically composed by attaching a chain of middleware functions and routers to a single `app` instance in one bootstrap file — the order middleware is registered in determines the order requests pass through it.

tour.title: Database Connection Pool
tour.description: backend/config/db.js configures a shared PostgreSQL connection pool from either a DATABASE_URL or discrete DB_* environment variables. It has the highest fan-in of any file in the codebase (14 dependents) — nearly every model in the data layer imports this single pool rather than opening its own connection, which is what lets the app scale its query load efficiently.
tour.languageLesson: Connection pooling (via node-postgres's `Pool`) reuses a fixed set of open database connections across concurrent requests instead of opening a new TCP connection per query, which is critical for Node.js server throughput.

tour.title: Middleware and Routing
tour.description: Every request server.js dispatches first passes through the middleware layer: authMiddleware verifies JWT bearer tokens, validationMiddleware rejects malformed input via express-validator, and errorHandler centralizes error formatting as the final middleware in the chain. Route files like personRoutes.js and familyRoutes.js then map specific URLs (matching the README's `/api/persons`, `/api/families` endpoints) to controller functions, attaching these middleware guards along the way.
tour.languageLesson: Express Router instances let each feature area define its own scoped set of routes and middleware, which the entry point then mounts onto a URL prefix (e.g. `app.use('/api/persons', personRoutes)`), keeping routing modular.

tour.title: Controllers
tour.description: Controllers are the next stop for a request: personController.js, familyController.js, and authController.js receive the validated, authenticated HTTP request and translate it into calls against the service layer, then map the results (or errors) back to HTTP status codes and JSON responses. They contain no SQL and no business rules themselves — that logic lives one layer deeper, in the services these controllers call.

tour.title: Service Layer
tour.description: Building on the controllers from Step 5, personService.js, familyService.js, and authService.js hold the actual business logic: authService.js hashes passwords with bcrypt and issues signed JWTs, while personService.js and familyService.js wrap their respective models with validation and orchestration rules. This is the layer the README's RBAC-via-email collaboration model and authentication flow are actually implemented in.

tour.title: GEDCOM Import & AI Biography
tour.description: Two of the README's headline features live here as dedicated domain logic. gedcomController.js and gedcomParser.js implement GEDCOM genealogy file import: the parser walks INDI/FAM records into structured persons and relationships, which the controller deduplicates and inserts inside a single database transaction. treeBuilder.js separately transforms stored persons/relationships into the ReactFlow-shaped nodes/edges the frontend renders, and aiController.js orchestrates AI-assisted biography generation by calling back into the person and relationship services from Step 6.
tour.languageLesson: GEDCOM is a decades-old genealogy text format organized into tagged INDI (individual) and FAM (family) record blocks; parsing it means walking a flat, indentation-based tree structure rather than JSON or XML.

tour.title: Data Access Models
tour.description: At the bottom of the backend's layered request lifecycle sit the models: personModel.js, familyModel.js, and userModel.js each expose parameterized SQL queries against a single table, using the connection pool from Step 3. Every service in Step 6 ultimately bottoms out in one of these files, keeping raw SQL confined to a single, testable layer rather than scattered through controllers.

tour.title: Database Schema
tour.description: schema.sql defines the normalized relational structure the models from Step 8 query against. The persons table (scoped to a family) is the core entity; the relationships table is a junction table linking two persons with a typed relationship such as parent or spouse; and families and users establish ownership and multi-tenancy. This is the concrete shape of the 'normalized PostgreSQL structure' the README promises.
tour.languageLesson: The relationships table's self-referencing foreign keys (person_id, related_person_id) with a check constraint preventing a person from relating to themselves is a classic pattern for modeling graph-like data in a relational database.

tour.title: Frontend Entry Point
tour.description: Switching to the client side of the monorepo: frontend/src/index.js mounts the root App component into the DOM using React 18's root API, and App.js sets up the router with lazily-loaded pages, a global toast provider, and network status detection. Together these mirror server.js's role on the backend — this is where the entire React application is assembled and started.
tour.languageLesson: React 18 introduced `createRoot` for concurrent rendering, replacing the older `ReactDOM.render` API; combined with `React.lazy`, route components can be code-split so each page's bundle only loads when the user navigates to it.

tour.title: Frontend Client Services
tour.description: api.js configures the shared Axios client with a base URL, a JWT bearer-token request interceptor, and a global response interceptor that redirects to login on 401s — it has the highest fan-in of any frontend file, since nearly every page and component in this tour funnels its HTTP calls through it. frontend/src/services/familyService.js then wraps that client with typed calls for families, persons, and relationships, mirroring the backend's family/person/relationship routes from Steps 4-5 on the client side.
tour.languageLesson: Axios interceptors let you inject cross-cutting concerns — auth headers, centralized error handling — into every request/response without repeating that logic in each API call site.

tour.title: Interactive Tree Visualization
tour.description: FamilyTree.js is the centerpiece of the README's 'Interactive Graphing' feature: it computes a generation-based hierarchical layout (adjacency building, generation assignment, collision resolution, iterative barycenter positioning) on top of React Flow, then renders the pannable, zoomable canvas with search highlighting and PNG export. PersonNode.js is the custom React Flow node component it renders for each family member, using generation-based color coding and connection handles to draw the tree's edges.
tour.languageLesson: React Flow lets you register custom `nodeTypes` components (like PersonNode) that receive position and data props from a layout algorithm — here a hand-rolled generation/barycenter layout instead of the more common Dagre auto-layout, giving finer control over how generations stack vertically.

tour.title: Feature Pages
tour.description: FamilyTreePage.js is the main workspace: it fetches tree data via the client services from Step 11, and assembles the FamilyTree canvas from Step 12 alongside search, GEDCOM import, activity feed, and collaboration panels into one page. PersonProfile.js is the largest page in the app, combining editable profile fields, a mini tree preview, memory and legacy-message CRUD, and the AI biography generation feature introduced back in Step 7.

tour.title: Containerization & Deployment
tour.description: docker-compose.yml orchestrates the three services the README's 'Docker-based production deployment' feature refers to: a PostgreSQL 15 database, the Express backend from Step 2, and the React frontend from Step 10, wired together with port mappings and a startup dependency chain. backend/Dockerfile builds a single-stage node:18-alpine image for the API, while frontend/Dockerfile uses a multi-stage build to compile the React app and then serve only the static output through a minimal Nginx image.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds (as in frontend/Dockerfile) use one stage with full build tooling to compile the app and a second, minimal runtime stage that only copies the compiled output — this keeps the final production image small since dev dependencies and build tools never ship.

tour.title: API Contract & Security Hardening
tour.description: swagger.yaml is the OpenAPI 3.0 contract documenting every endpoint this tour has walked through in code, from POST /api/auth/register through POST /api/gedcom/import — the same list the README summarizes as its API surface. security_audit.md closes the tour with the project's own accounting of hardening work applied before production: removing a hardcoded JWT fallback secret, tightening wildcard CORS, adding XSS sanitization, and locking down rate limiting and Helmet configuration across the middleware and entry point covered in Steps 2-4.
tour.languageLesson: An OpenAPI/Swagger spec is a machine-readable contract for a REST API — tools like swagger-ui-express can render it as interactive documentation directly from the YAML, keeping docs and the implemented routes from drifting apart.
